analyst_observations: Skill achieves 100% pass rate vs 87.5% baseline — the 12.5% gap comes from two structural assertions: subagent spawning (architect + critic) and formal approval points in design-review-package.md
Without the skill, the agent produces comparable content quality (even more verbose in some cases — 15 decisions vs 7) but lacks the structured review process (no critic, no formal approval points)
Rejection test (eval-3) is non-discriminating — both with and without skill correctly reject incomplete inputs at 100% pass rate
Skill costs ~20% more tokens (58K vs 49K) primarily due to subagent overhead (architect + critic spawning)
Duration difference is modest (+8.5%) — subagents run in parallel so the overhead is mostly from critic review, not doubled wall-clock time

The without_skill agent produced a stage-4-handoff.md for neither eval, while with_skill produced it for both — this indicates the skill template structure drives completeness of the full artifact set
Qualitative advantage of the skill: formal approval points with options/recommendations/questions (structured for user decision-making) vs informal 'open questions' in without_skill output
